pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh mandate and verifier.py docstrings.
Verified against setup_task.sh mandate stating 'UNEP DIVISION OF EARLY WARNING AND ASSESSMENT'.
Verified against setup_task.sh SLP_FILE and PRATE_FILE variables.
Verified against setup_task.sh mandate requirements and verifier.py scoring criteria.

Verified through Python script analysis of NCEP-NCAR Reanalysis 1 data: the Azores High (high SLP) has significantly lower precipitation compared to the ITCZ (low SLP). Explicitly required by verifier.py scoring.
Verified against setup_task.sh mandate explicitly requesting 'Time Step: July' and verifier.py check for 'july'.

privileged_info_summary: The data originates from the real NCEP-NCAR Reanalysis 1 dataset containing monthly long-term mean sea level pressure (slp) and precipitation rate (prate). Analysis of this data for July confirms that high SLP regions, such as the Azores High, exhibit significantly lower precipitation compared to low SLP regions like the ITCZ, validating the expected NEGATIVE relationship. The task requires exporting visual plots of these global maps using NASA Panoply and drafting an assessment report to the exact output paths specified in the mandate.
